perturbation.original_text: 14. DEFINITIONS "APPLICATION" means the English version of the E.piphany Application programs listed in Exhibit A, in object code format, any accompanying Documentation, and any Updates (as defined in Section 5.5 ("Updates"), only if and when generally commercially available as part of the Maintenance Services provided pursuant to Section 5 ("Maintenance Services").
perturbation.changed_text: 14. DEFINITIONS "APPLICATION" means the E.piphany Application programs listed in Exhibit A, in object code format, any accompanying Documentation.
perturbation.explanation: The clause 'English version' from the definition of 'Application' has been removed. This creates a contradiction, because Section 1.4 ('LICENSE RESTRICTIONS') states that 'HSNS acknowledges and agrees that the rights granted herein are solely to the English version of the Application.' By removing 'English version' from the definition, it's unclear if non-English versions are now permitted, contradicting Section 1.4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 LICENSE RESTRICTIONS. Except as expressly provided herein, HSNS shall not (i) rent, lease, loan, sell or otherwise distribute the Application, or any modification thereto, in whole or in part; (ii) cause or permit reverse engineering, reverse compilation, unauthorized access or assembly of all or any portion of the Application; (iii) allow any outsourcing or application service providers to access and use the Application as Outsourcing Customers, (iv) publish the results of Application performance benchmarks to any third party without E.piphany's express written consent; (v) export the Application in violation of U.S. Department of Commerce export administration regulations; and (vi) except as otherwise expressly allowed herein, permit any third party or unlicensed user or computer system to access or use the Application. HSNS agrees that it shall only provide the Application in combination with and included in Outsourcing Services HSNS acknowledges and agrees that the rights granted herein are solely to the English version of the Application. All rights not expressly granted hereunder are reserved to E.piphany.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined "APPLICATION" as the 'English version' of the application, which aligned with Section 1.4 stating that rights are solely to the 'English version'. The changed text removes 'English version' from the definition of "APPLICATION". This omission creates a contradiction with Section 1.4, because it is now unclear whether the license extends to non-English versions, conflicting with the explicit restriction in Section 1.4, creating a clear and direct conflict and contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: HSNS shall meet with E.piphany on a quarterly basis at a mutually agreed to date and time to discuss non-binding forecasts. HSNS shall provide E.piphany with a non-binding, rolling six-month forecast report in advance of the quarterly meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: HSNS shall meet with E.piphany to discuss non-binding forecasts.
perturbation.explanation: The phrases 'on a quarterly basis at a mutually agreed to date and time' and 'HSNS shall provide E.piphany with a non-binding, rolling six-month forecast report in advance of the quarterly meeting' has been removed. By omitting the requirement for quarterly meetings and the submission of forecast reports, the modified text now introduces ambiguity regarding the frequency and format of forecast discussions. This omission directly contradicts the reference to 'Annual Reports' in Section 2.1, which are used to determine Year 2 and Year 3 Quarterly Payments, as defined in Exhibit 'A,' which depends on accurate forcast information. Therefore, the value of 'Year 2 Quarterly Payments' and 'Year 3 Quarterly Payments' can not be properly calculated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 ANNUAL REPORTS. On the thirtieth (30th) day after the end of each of the first two years of this Agreement, HSNS shall submit to E.piphany a report (in a form provided by E.piphany and reasonably acceptable to HSNS) listing&bbsp;(i) the total number of emails distributed for Years 1 and 2 and (ii) the Application with which the Outsourcing Services are rendered. Year 2 and Year 3 Quarterly Payments, as defined in Exhibit "A," shall be based upon these annual reports. All reporting and payment requirements under this Section shall be subject to the audit requirements of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the requirement for quarterly meetings and forecast reports. This contradicts Section 2.1, which states that 'Year 2 and Year 3 Quarterly Payments, as defined in Exhibit "A," shall be based upon these annual reports.' Since the changed text removes the obligation to provide forecast reports (which are used to generate the annual reports), it is impossible to determine the 'Year 2 Quarterly Payments' and 'Year 3 Quarterly Payments' as per Section 2.1, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue for a term set forth in Exhibit B, and may be renewed for successive one (1) year terms pursuant to mutual written agreement of the parties, unless terminated earlier pursuant to this Section 12.1.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue for a term set forth in Exhibit B, unless terminated earlier pursuant to this Section 12.1.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and may be renewed for successive one (1) year terms pursuant to mutual written agreement of the parties', the amended clause eliminates any mention of renewal terms. This creates an inconsistency with Exhibit A, specifically section 5.0, which outlines 'TERM. This Agreement shall have an initial term of three (3) years.'. Without the renewal option in section 12.1, the continuation of the agreement beyond the initial three years becomes uncertain, making the long-term investment and planning assumptions based on an extended relationship invalid and subject to dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.0. TERM. This Agreement shall have an initial term of three (3) years.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the renewal clause in Section 12.1 creates a direct contradiction with Section 5.0, which specifies an initial term of three years. The original text allowed for renewals, implying a possible continuation beyond the initial term. The changed text removes this possibility, creating uncertainty about the agreement's duration beyond three years and contradicting the implied continuation of the relationship. Without renewal terms, Exhibit A, section 5.0 outlining TERM is left in conflict.
